title: 10 best herbal teas for reddened corners in mouth
intro_desc: Herbal teas for reddened corners in mouth are natural remedies that help soothe and calm irritated areas around the mouth, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. The best herbal teas for this condition include Echinacea purpurea, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Zingiber officinale, Calendula officinalis, Mentha x piperita, Achillea millefolium, Melissa officinalis, Aloe barbadensis, Chamomilla recutita, and Hydrastis canadensis. If left untreated, reddened corners in mouth can lead to painful eating, speaking difficulties, and social isolation due to embarrassment and discomfort.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tea is a natural remedy that can help with reddened corners in the mouth. This happens when the corners of the mouth get irritated and become inflamed, often due to cold or windy weather, or even dry air. Echinacea purpurea tea is rich in antioxidants, which help to soothe and calm the irritated skin. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties reduce swelling and ease pain, making it easier to eat and speak. The antiseptic properties of the tea prevent infection and promote healing, helping to speed up the recovery process. Drinking echinacea purpurea tea regularly can also boost the immune system, making it less likely for the condition to occur again in the future.

Mentha x piperita tea, also known as peppermint tea, can help soothe and calm redness in the mouth. This is because peppermint has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and ease pain. When you drink peppermint tea, the menthol in it can numb the area and provide a cooling sensation that can help calm the irritation. Peppermint tea can also help to reduce bad breath and kill bacteria that can cause mouth infections, which can further contribute to redness and discomfort. Additionally, peppermint tea can help to relax the muscles in your mouth and jaw, which can become tense and cause pain when you have mouth sores or irritation.

Glycyrrhiza glabra tea, also known as licorice root tea, has been used to help soothe and heal mouth ulcers and reddened corners in the mouth. This is because it contains powerful anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain. The tea's active compound, glycyrrhizin, has been shown to have a soothing effect on the mucous membranes in the mouth, which can become irritated and inflamed due to factors such as gum disease, poor oral hygiene, or certain medications. Drinking licorice root tea may help to calm the inflammation and promote healing, making it easier to recover from mouth ulcers and reddened corners. Additionally, the tea's antimicrobial properties can help to combat bacterial and fungal infections that can exacerbate mouth ulcers. As a result, drinking licorice root tea may provide relief from the discomfort and pain associated with mouth ulcers and reddened corners in the mouth.

Zingiber officinale tea, also known as ginger tea, is a natural remedy for reddened corners in the mouth, often caused by gingivitis or inflammation. The anti-inflammatory properties in ginger help reduce swelling and ease pain. Ginger also has antibacterial properties that fight off germs and bacteria that cause infection and irritation. When you drink ginger tea, the active compounds in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, are absorbed into the bloodstream and travel to the mouth, where they work to calm down the inflammation and reduce redness. Additionally, ginger has natural antiseptic properties that help prevent infection and promote healing, making it an effective remedy for soothing reddened corners in the mouth.

Melissa officinalis, also known as lemon balm, is a herbal tea that can help with reddened corners in the mouth. This condition is often caused by inflammation, which can be due to irritation from spicy food, dry mouth, or other factors. The antibacterial properties in lemon balm tea can help reduce the bacteria that cause inflammation and infection. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties can help calm down the irritated areas and reduce redness. The soothing effect of lemon balm tea can also provide relief from discomfort and pain. Moreover, lemon balm has antiviral properties that can help fight off viruses that may be contributing to the condition. Drinking lemon balm tea can help to reduce the severity of the symptoms and promote healing in the affected areas.

Achillea millefolium tea, also known as yarrow tea, is a natural remedy that can help soothe and calm reddened corners in the mouth. This is because yarrow tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and ease pain. The tea is made from the flowers and leaves of the yarrow plant, which contain compounds that have been shown to have antiseptic and antibacterial properties, helping to combat infection and prevent further irritation. When consumed, yarrow tea can help to bring down the inflammation and redness in the mouth, making it a good remedy for mouth ulcers, canker sores, and other mouth irritations. Drinking yarrow tea can also help to promote healing and speed up the recovery process, making it a popular natural remedy for mouth health. Yarrow tea can be made by steeping the dried flowers and leaves in hot water, and can be consumed as a warm tea several times a day to help alleviate symptoms.

Aloe barbadensis tea is a natural remedy that can help soothe reddened corners in the mouth. The tea is made from the gel of the aloe vera plant, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and calm irritation. When you drink the tea, the anti-inflammatory compounds are absorbed into the body and can help ease pain and discomfort in the mouth. The tea can also help to reduce bacterial growth, which can cause infections and make mouth sores worse. Additionally, aloe vera has antibacterial and antifungal properties that can help prevent the spread of infection and promote healing. Aloe barbadensis tea can be especially helpful when you have mouth sores or canker sores, as it can help to reduce pain, inflammation, and discomfort. When you drink the tea, the soothing properties can help to calm the area and promote healing, which can lead to a faster recovery.

Calendula officinalis tea is good for reddened corners in the mouth because it has anti-inflammatory properties. When the mouth corners turn red and sore, it's usually because of irritation or infection. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and ease pain. This makes it easier to heal the area. The tea also has antimicrobial properties, which kill bacteria and other germs that can cause infections. This helps prevent the spread of infection and promotes healing. By soothing and protecting the mouth corners, Calendula officinalis tea can help you feel better and recover faster.

Hydrastis canadensis, also known as goldenseal, is a plant with many health benefits. When made into a tea, it can help soothe and calm the mucous membranes in the mouth. The active compound berberine in goldenseal has antimicrobial properties, which can help fight off the bacteria that cause infections and inflammation in the mouth. This can be especially helpful for people who experience redness and irritation in the corners of their mouth, often a sign of a fungal or bacterial infection. Goldenseal tea can help reduce swelling and ease discomfort, allowing the affected area to heal faster. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties can help calm the skin and reduce redness, making it a potential remedy for mouth corners that are red and irritated.

Aloe vera tea is a natural remedy for reddened corners in the mouth, also known as angular cheilitis. This condition is often caused by dryness, irritation, or infection. Drinking aloe vera tea can help soothe and calm the inflamed area. Aloe vera has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and pain. It also contains vitamins and minerals, such as vitamin E and zinc, which promote healing and protect the skin from further irritation. The antibacterial properties in aloe vera tea can help combat infections and prevent them from spreading. Additionally, aloe vera tea is gentle and non-irritating, making it an excellent choice for sensitive areas.
